content.full_text:
April 26, 1995


Honorable Shirley A. Morton
City Councilmember
City of Calimesa
10961 Desert Lawn Drive, #15
Calimesa, CA 92320

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-138

Dear Councilmember Morton:

You have requested additional advice regarding your duties as
a Calimesa City Councilmember under the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ This letter
is intended to supplement the advice provided to you in our letter
of March 30, 1995. (Morton Advice Letter, No. I-95-092.)

In your letter requesting the advice you received in the
Morton Advice Letter, supra, you stated that you are a member of
the Golden State Mobile Home Owners League (GSMOL) and serve as
associate manager for region nine (Riverside County) and as the
GSMOL president of your mobile home park. As an associate manager
you travel to other cities and county areas to discuss mobile home
issues. We advised that so long as you did not receive any
payments from the GSMOL, you would not have an economic
interest. (Section 87103(c).) Since you received our last
advice letter you have been appointed as regional manager for
region nine of the GSMOL. You are now considering accepting
travel reimbursement from the GSMOL.

As we discussed previously, Section 87100 of the Act
prohibits you from making, participating in making, or otherwise
using your official position to influence a decision if it is

___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-95-138
Page 2


reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public
generally, on any source of income aggregating $250 or more in
value provided to, received by or promised to you within 12 months
prior to the time when the decision is made. Thus, if you receive
$250 from GSMOL, you would be prohibited from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using your official position
to influence any decision that would foreseeably and materially
effect GSMOL.2

You have not provided facts pertaining to any specific
decision. If you do receive $250 or more from GSMOL and are
confronted with a decision that may affect GSMOL, you should
contact us for further advice.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By:) John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division


_________________________

2 Section 82030(b)(2) provides that "income" does not include
"reimbursement for travel expenses and per diem received from a
bona fide educational, academic, or charitable organization."
Consistent with the Internal Revenue Code, we have interpreted
"bona fide educational, academic, or charitable organization" as
those organizations formed under Internal Revenue Code Section
501(c)(3). This would not apply to a nonprofit civic organization
such as the GSMOL. (See, Abt Advice Letter, No. I-91-436;
Internal Revenue Code Section 501(c)(4).)

sections.question_synthetic: Does a City Councilmember's acceptance of travel reimbursement from the Golden State Mobile Home Owners League create a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act, and would such reimbursement constitute an economic interest that triggers disclosure and recusal requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: If the councilmember receives $250 or more in travel reimbursement from GSMOL within 12 months, GSMOL becomes an economic interest under Section 87100, and the councilmember would be prohibited from making or influencing any decision that would foreseeably and materially affect GSMOL. The councilmember should seek further advice if confronted with a specific decision affecting GSMOL.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a follow-up advice letter supplementing a prior letter (No. 1-95-092 dated March 30, 1995). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers, but question and conclusion are clearly identified in the narrative. Advice is conditional pending receipt of facts about a specific decision.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does a City Councilmember's acceptance of travel reimbursement from the Golden State Mobile Home Owners League create a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act, and would such reimbursement constitute an economic interest that triggers disclosure and recusal requirements?

CONCLUSION: If the councilmember receives $250 or more in travel reimbursement from GSMOL within 12 months, GSMOL becomes an economic interest under Section 87100, and the councilmember would be prohibited from making or influencing any decision that would foreseeably and materially affect GSMOL. The councilmember should seek further advice if confronted with a specific decision affecting GSMOL.

embedding.summary: The FPPC provides supplemental advice to Calimesa City Councilmember Shirley A. Morton regarding conflict-of-interest duties under the Political Reform Act, specifically addressing whether accepting travel reimbursement from the Golden State Mobile Home Owners League would constitute a material economic interest triggering recusal requirements.
